data.reasoningText:
This project uses the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner (specified in global.json). For this runner, the TRX report is produced via a command-line switch. Let me run the tests with the TRX report logger.

With Microsoft.Testing.Platform, the way to produce TRX is:
`dotnet test --logger trx`

Let me run it.

data.content: The project needs to opt into the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner and add the TRX report extension. Let me update the project file.
data.reasoningText:
The project is using Microsoft.Testing.Platform as specified in global.json, but the project itself isn't configured for it. With MSTest 3.8.0 and the Microsoft.Testing.Platform runner, the project needs to opt in. Let me check how to enable MTP in the project.

For MSTest 3.8+, you need to set `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>` or `<UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>true</UseMicrosoftTestingPlatformRunner>` in the project file.


Actually, I need to verify the exact configuration. With MSTest 3.4 and later, I should enable the MSTest runner by setting `<EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>` and changing the output type to `Exe`. I'll also need to add the TRX report package for test result generation.

The global.json is already configured to use Microsoft.Testing.Platform as the test runner, so I need to make sure the project file aligns with that setup.

toolResult.textResultForLlm:
Contents of https://aka.ms/dotnet-test/mtp:
**This article applies to:** ✔️ .NET 6 SDK and later versions

## Name

`dotnet test` - .NET test driver used to execute unit tests.

## Description

The `dotnet test` command builds the solution and runs the tests with either VSTest or Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). The test runner you use determines the available command-line options and behavior.

Note

Test runner selection is available starting with .NET 10 SDK. In earlier versions of .NET, tests are always executed with VSTest.

### Choosing a test runner

To enable Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), you need to specify the test runner in the [`global.json`](global-json) file:

```
{
    "test": {
        "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"
    }
}
```

Note

`VSTest` is a valid value for test runner. It is the current default and can be omitted.

Important

The `dotnet test` experience for MTP is only supported in `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 and later.

### Test runner documentation

The available command-line options, behavior, and capabilities differ depending on which test runner you use:

*   **[dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)** - The traditional test platform, available in .NET 6 SDK and later. This is the default and only test runner in versions earlier than .NET 10 SDK. Provides comprehensive test discovery, filtering, and result reporting capabilities.
    
*   **[dotnet test with MTP](dotnet-test-mtp)** - The modern testing platform, available in .NET 10 SDK and later. Offers faster test execution and more flexible test module selection.
    

## See also

*   [Testing with dotnet test](../testing/unit-testing-with-dotnet-test)
*   [dotnet test with VSTest](dotnet-test-vstest)
*   [dotnet test with MTP](dotnet-test-mtp)
*   [Frameworks and Targets](../../standard/frameworks)
*   [.NET Runtime Identifier (RID) catalog](../rid-catalog)

**This article applies to:** ✔️ .NET 6 SDK and later versions

## Name

`dotnet test` - .NET test driver used to execute unit tests.

## Description

The `dotnet test` command builds the solution and runs the tests with either VSTest or Microsoft Testing Platform (MTP). The test runner you use determines the available command-line options and behavior.

Test runner selection is available starting with .NET 10 SDK. In earlier versions of .NET, tests are always executed with VSTest.

### Choosing a test runner

To enable Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP), you need to specify the test runner in the [`global.json`](global-json) file:

{
    "test": {
        "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform"

`VSTest` is a valid value for test runner. It is the current default and can be omitted.

Important

The `dotnet test` experience for MTP is only supported in `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 and later.

### Test runner documentation

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The docs say MTP support in `dotnet test` requires `Microsoft.Testing.Platform` version 1.7 or later, but MSTest 3.8.0 brings in version 1.6.2. I need to upgrade the Microsoft.Testing.Platform to 1.7+.

Let me check what versions are available.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet package search Microsoft.Testing.Platform --take 5 2>&1 | head -20", "description": "Search for latest MTP version", "initial_wait": 30}

data.reasoningText:
The latest Microsoft.Testing.Platform version is 2.2.2. I need to upgrade the MSTest and related packages to get version 1.7+ of the testing platform. Let me also check what MSTest version brings in MTP 1.7+, or I can just explicitly add the newer version of Microsoft.Testing.Platform.

Actually, the simplest approach might be to upgrade MSTest to a newer version that brings in MTP 1.7+. Or I can explicitly add a package reference to Microsoft.Testing.Platform 1.7+.

Let me try upgrading MSTest to a version that ships with MTP >= 1.7. Let me search for the latest MSTest version.

MSTest 4.2.2 is available. Let me upgrade MSTest to 4.2.2 and also upgrade the TrxReport extension. With MSTest 4.x, the Microsoft.Testing.Platform version should be >= 1.7.

Let me also check the latest TrxReport version.

          Define the level of the verbosity for the --diagnostic.

          Do not report non successful exit value for specific exit codes

          Display .NET test application information.

          List available tests.
  
      --minimum-expected-tests
          Specifies the minimum number of tests that are expected to run.

          The directory where the test results are going to be placed.
          If the specified directory doesn't exist, it's created.
          The default is TestResults in the directory that contains the test application.
  
      --timeout
          A global test execution timeout.
          Takes one argument as string in the format <value>[h|m|s] where 'value' is float.
  
  
  Extension options:
      --coverage
          Collect the code coverage using dotnet-coverage tool.
  
      --coverage-output
          The output of the generated code coverage report.
          If only filename is specified then the coverage report will be generated in the '--results-directory' directory.

          Output file format. Supported values: 'coverage', 'xml' and 'cobertura'

          The path to the code coverage XML settings file.

          Filters tests using the given expression. For more information, see the Filter option details section. For more information and examples on how to use selective unit test filtering, see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/selective-unit-tests.

          Specifies a maximum number of test failures that, when exceeded, will abort the test run.
  
      --no-ansi
          Disable outputting ANSI escape characters to screen.
  
      --no-progress
          Disable reporting progress to screen.
  
      --output
          Output verbosity when reporting tests.
          Valid values are 'Normal', 'Detailed'. Default is 'Normal'.
  
      --report-trx
          Enable generating TRX report

          The name of the generated TRX report
  
      --settings
          The path, relative or absolute, to the .runsettings file. For more information and examples on how to configure test run, see https://learn.microsoft.com/visualstudio/test/configure-unit-tests-by-using-a-dot-runsettings-file#the-runsettings-file
  
      --show-stderr
          Determines when to show captured error output of a test.
          Valid values are 'All', 'Failed', 'None'. Default is 'All'.
  
      --show-stdout
          Determines when to show captured standard output of a test.
          Valid values are 'All', 'Failed', 'None'. Default is 'All'.

          Specify or override a key-value pair parameter. For more information and examples, see https://learn.microsoft.com/visualstudio/test/configure-unit-tests-by-using-a-dot-runsettings-file#testrunparameters

**Changes made to `TestProject.csproj`:**
- Upgraded `MSTest` from 3.8.0 → 4.2.2 (required MTP ≥ 1.7)
- Added `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType`, and `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` properties
- Added `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport` package for TRX support

The MTP runner uses `--report-trx` (passed after `--`) instead of `--logger trx`.
